popularStory.headline: Elizabeth Jenks
popularStory.description:
Elizabeth Jenks was born in 1510. She was the daughter of William Jenks and Elizabeth Adams. She married Richard Rich, 1st Baron Rich. She died on 12 December 1558.2 Her married name became Rich.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth Jenkes於大約1504年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，她的父親William Jenkes25歲，母親Elizabeth Adams23歲。她和Sir Richard Rich至少有2個兒子和11個女兒。她於1537年住在英國英格蘭爾赫夫。她去世於1558年12月16日在英國米德萊可倫敦享年55歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可洛福德。

nameMeanings.name: Jenkes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Worcestershire): patronymic, with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s, from a pet form of the personal names Jukel, Jokel, which are from Breton Judicael and this one from Old Breton Iudicael. The Breton personal name is derived from iuth ‘lord’ with a suffix -ig + hael, hail ‘generous, bountiful’. In England the personal name was particularly common in Yorkshire and Lincolnshire and in districts where Bretons settled after the Norman Conquest. Compare Jewell .

variants.name: Jewell
meanings: English (Devon and Cornwall): from an Old Breton personal name, Iudhael, composed of elements meaning ‘lord’ + ‘generous, bountiful’ (see Jewkes ), which was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, a king of Brittany who abdicated and spent the last part of his life in a monastery. Forms of this name are found in medieval records not only in Devon and Cornwall, where they are of native origin, but also in East Anglia and even Yorkshire, where they were imported by Bretons after the Norman Conquest. The name was seemingly reintroduced from France in the 17th century by Huguenots.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth Jenkes was born about 1504, in London, Middlesex, England, her father, William Jenkes, was 25 and her mother, Elizabeth Adams, was 23. She had at least 2 sons and 11 daughters with Sir Richard Rich. She lived in Hough, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom in 1537. She died on 16 December 1558, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 55, and was buried in Rochford, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 11 filles avec Sir Richard Rich. Elle a vécu à Hough, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni en 1537. Elle est décédée le 16 décembre 1558, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Rochford, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth Jenkes(이)가 대략 1504년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Jenkes(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Adams(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 Sir Richard Rich(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 1537년도에 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 허프에서 살았다. 그녀는 1558년 12월 16일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 록h퍼드에 묻혔다.
